trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade. Canada exports crude oil, natural gas, aircraft, precious metals, and agricultural products to the UK. The UK exports machinery, pharmaceuticals, vehicles, and financial services to Canada. Post-Brexit, both are negotiating a Canada-UK FTA.

diplomatic.summary: Canada and the UK share the deepest possible ties — Commonwealth, Five Eyes intelligence alliance, shared monarch (until 2023), parliamentary democracy tradition, and common law system. Both are NATO allies and G7 members. The relationship is one of the strongest in Canadian foreign policy.

military.summary: Both are Five Eyes partners, NATO allies, and CANZUK security partners. Extensive military cooperation including joint operations, equipment interoperability, intelligence sharing, and combined training exercises. UK and Canada cooperate closely in Arctic security.

financial.summary: London is a major financial center for Canadian companies and banks. RBC, TD, and other Canadian banks have major UK operations. British companies invest extensively in Canadian energy, mining, and finance. Bilateral investment is among the largest in the world.

energy.summary: UK-Canada energy cooperation deepened post-Brexit and post-Ukraine war. Canada is increasing LNG exports to UK. British energy companies (BP, Shell) have major Canadian upstream investments. Growing green hydrogen cooperation.

scientific.summary: Outstanding scientific partnership. UK-Canada universities exchange large numbers of researchers. Joint funding through UKRI-NSERC programs. Collaboration in AI (Toronto-London AI corridor), quantum computing, materials science, and health research. Joint space programs.
